name: Rassier School Bus Svc Inc
principals: Sandra D. Washburn (listed President; deceased per local obituary); prior owners Edward & Muriel Rassier; Tim Washburn (spouse) referenced as co-operator
districts_or_customers: Hill City Public Schools / Aitkin County, MN area (rural northern MN; primary local district)
recent_news: Yellow Pages directory listing flags location as CLOSED; obituary references Sandra Washburn who is listed as President in source data, suggesting a death-of-principal / succession event. Status of operating entity uncertain (2024-2026).
fit_notes: Sub-scale single-yard rural MN operator (~9 buses) with apparent principal-death succession event - high motivation but very small and outside Summit's typical density zones; tuck-in attractiveness is limited unless used as bolt-on to an existing MN footprint. Verify operating status before any outreach.

name: To & From School Trnsprtn LLC
districts_or_customers: Likely Norwalk-area private/parochial schools or SPED routes; Norwalk Public Schools' primary contractor is a different vendor (ECS Transportation referenced)
recent_news: No M&A, safety, or news coverage located. No active website or distinct online presence identified - typical of a very small CT LLC operating under Connecticut's 7D/SPV special pupil vehicle framework.
fit_notes: Very small Fairfield County CT operator (~14 employees, ~$0.6M revenue) likely running SPED/7D vans rather than full-size school buses - low strategic value as a standalone tuck-in; could be additive only if Summit is building a CT special-needs cluster. Ownership unverified - needs direct outreach/CT SOTS lookup.

name: M & C School Bus LLC
districts_or_customers: Likely Everett MA area private/charter or sub-contract work; Everett Public Schools' primary contract is held by Durham School Services (Summit/National Express platform), so M&C is not the prime EPS contractor
recent_news: No M&A, safety, or news coverage located. No website, no public listings beyond directory data; typical profile of a Massachusetts 7D-vehicle / van operator.
fit_notes: Very small Boston-North operator (~12 employees, ~$0.6M revenue), almost certainly 7D vans rather than yellow-bus contracts. Since Durham (Summit platform) already holds the Everett prime contract, M&C is more likely a sub-contractor or private-school feeder - marginal tuck-in value; needs MA SOC lookup for principal ID.

name: Gary Aisquith Bus Lines Inc
districts_or_customers: Anne Arundel County Public Schools area (Riva/South County); note AACPS prime contracts in this geography are held by Annapolis Bus Company and R.E. Wilson (both owned by Student Transportation of America), so Aisquith likely operates charter / activity / private-school runs rather than a major district route package
recent_news: BBB A+ rating (non-accredited); BBB file opened 2012 with one minor complaint (bed-bug allegation on a bus). No M&A activity. Surrounding AACPS market saw a 2025 bus-driver contract / strike-vote process at STA-owned vendors.
fit_notes: Eponymous family-owned charter/school bus operator in Anne Arundel County - small (~13 employees) but operates inside Summit's MD footprint and in a market dominated by STA. Limited tuck-in scale on its own, but useful as a bolt-on if Summit is building a MD/DC capital-region charter+SPED cluster; reachable owner-operator with likely succession appetite.

name: Kidsmobile Inc
principals: G. Paul Malloch (listed Owner per source data)
districts_or_customers: Address 26314 Center Ridge Rd is in Westlake OH (not city of Cleveland) - implies service to Westside Cleveland suburbs (Westlake, Bay Village, Rocky River, North Olmsted, Fairview Park); likely private/parochial school routes given small size vs. district-run public transportation in those suburbs
recent_news: No M&A, safety, or news coverage located. No public website, no LinkedIn presence, no Ohio SOS results surfaced in open web - very low online footprint typical of a small private-school van operator.
fit_notes: Very small Westside Cleveland suburban operator (~13 employees, ~$0.6M revenue), almost certainly private/parochial school + small-vehicle routes. Marginal standalone tuck-in - relevant only as additive density if Summit is consolidating a Northeast Ohio private/parochial cluster. Verify principal status (Malloch) and Ohio SOS filings before outreach.
